mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS path lowers real discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is flat or falling, because disinflation preserves the real rate decline and reduces the risk of renewed tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is declining month-over-month, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent low indicates labor-market deterioration that compresses earnings expectations and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be visible when PAYEMS is also slowing, because weaker payroll growth confirms that unemployment is not a temporary noise move.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency when PAYEMS growth is below its 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and PAYEMS identifies broad nominal demand and income growth that supports sales and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment, which implies demand growth is not translating into durable labor income strength.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and PAYEMS expansions do not outperform baseline SPX returns over 60d, or if the signal becomes negative when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving industrial supply and profit leverage, which supports cyclical equity gains over the next 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when HOUST is also turning up, because housing recovery confirms that the production upswing is broad rather than sector-specific.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive following INDPRO acceleration, or if the signal disappears when HOUST is not improving.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST reflects a housing-cycle impulse that boosts consumption, construction activity, and forward earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if housing starts rise while consumer spending weakens, because then the housing signal is not propagating into aggregate demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX returns are negative in most housing-upturn episodes, or if the effect vanishes when RSAFS is below its 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still elevated, the market should price a restrictive policy overhang that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if inflation is cooling quickly, because then high nominal policy rates are less restrictive in real terms.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes do not coincide with negative 20d SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive when CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 3-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A continuing decline in PAYEMS indicates weakening employment momentum and lower household income growth, which should reduce earnings visibility and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days. The effect is falsified if payroll declines occur without any contemporaneous rise in unemployment, because then the labor signal is too small to matter for equities.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal weakens to zero when UNRATE is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies genuine real-activity acceleration, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger earnings expectations. The mechanism fails when GDP rises but retail sales do not, because then the growth impulse is not broad enough to transmit into listed-company revenues.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior readings
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-sales improvements do not produce above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the signal is not positive when both series are above their recent averages.
